title: CRITICAL: bd/markdown shadow-sync is backwards from CLAUDE.md's stated architecture
description:
Discovered 2026-07-23 during a critical repo audit (4 parallel agents: CUJ/objectives, research alignment, beads usage, CLAUDE.md consistency).

CLAUDE.md's 'Beads issue tracker' section says: markdown task files' Status headers 'are shadow-synced into bd, not the other way around' -- implying bd is authoritative and markdown is a one-way input mirror at most, ideally read-only. AGENTS.md goes further, claiming Status headers are 'frozen display, no longer scanned' -- implying markdown doesn't even feed bd anymore.

Neither is what agentic/shadow.py actually does. Its own docstring says 'Markdown stays the source of truth; bd mirrors the queue,' and sync() force-imports every row with allow_stale=True ('Markdown is the source of truth: force every row in, even over a...'), which makes markdown win for ~90% of issues on any re-sync (302 of 336 in this repo per the audit). Concretely: a bd-side close of any md-* issue is silently reverted the next time shadow-sync runs, and the two cross-repo cutover workflow scripts (.claude/workflows/cross-repo-beads-adoption.js:56-58, full-cutover-and-health-check.js:66) both document re-running this exact sync as a normal operation -- with no guard anywhere against reopening already-closed work.

This is the same root cause behind a three-way doc contradiction: AGENTS.md:28/111 say Status headers are 'frozen, no longer scanned' while token-discipline.md:26 tells agents to grep '^Status:' as if it's live data, and CLAUDE.md says shadow-synced-into-bd as if scanning happens but only flows one way (md->bd, not the reverse) -- all three are describing different intended designs, none matching shipped code.

Also found: shadow.py's bd_import upserts and never prunes (a renamed task file mints a new md-* id and orphans the old one, unguarded), and an unrecognized Status value silently maps to 'blocked' instead of erroring. Neither /work nor /drain SKILL.md mentions markdown Status or shadow-sync at all (zero grep hits), so nothing tells a worker that closing an md-* issue in bd isn't durable.

Needs a real design decision, not a quick patch: is markdown still meant to be writable/authoritative for spec-file-originated tasks (in which case bd's close there needs to survive re-sync, or closes should write back to markdown), or is bd now fully authoritative (in which case shadow-sync should become import-once / bd-wins, not markdown-wins)? Whichever way, fix shadow.py's allow_stale=True default, add prune-on-rename and error-on-unrecognized-status, add a guard against the cross-repo workflow scripts' re-sync silently reopening closed issues, and reconcile AGENTS.md/CLAUDE.md/token-discipline.md to describe the same actual behavior.
acceptance_criteria: A design decision is recorded (in CLAUDE.md's Beads section or a new docs/decisions/ entry) on markdown-vs-bd authority for spec-file-originated tasks; shadow.py's sync direction matches that decision (not blindly allow_stale=True); a regression test proves a bd-side close of an md-* issue survives a shadow-sync re-run OR is documented as intentionally not surviving; AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md, and token-discipline.md agree on whether Status headers are still live/scanned.

title: CRITICAL: agentic init's controlled bd-init can un-commit unrelated pushed work
description: Discovered 2026-07-23 during the ~/automation bd cutover. agentic/initialize.py's _controlled_bd_init() runs bd init then unconditionally 'git reset HEAD~1' to undo bd's own auto-commit, assuming bd init's commit is always exactly the new HEAD. In ~/automation this assumption was false: bd init's commit landed on top of an already-pushed, unrelated commit (64b7d5b, 'docs: close manual-fix open items as won't-fix'), and the reset silently un-committed THAT real work back into the working tree (still present, not lost, but uncommitted and at risk of being discarded by any subsequent hard-reset/checkout). The worker caught it via reflog and it was reconciled cleanly during a push rebase this time -- but the mechanism is a live landmine: any repo where something else commits between the start of agentic init and its internal bd-init call (a concurrent process, a pre-commit hook, bd's own hooks writing a commit first) risks this same silent un-commit, and a future run might not get caught/recovered as luckily. Fix: _controlled_bd_init must verify the commit it's about to reset IS bd's own auto-commit (e.g. check the commit message/author matches bd's known pattern, or capture the exact SHA bd's init call produces and reset only if HEAD still equals that SHA) before resetting, and abort loudly instead of resetting blind if that check fails.
close_reason: Fixed: _controlled_bd_init now verifies HEAD actually advanced and that the new commit is bd's own (subject + parent-SHA check) before resetting; aborts loudly instead of resetting blind otherwise. TDD: tests/test_agentic_initialize.py (3 tests), full check.sh green including the roundtrip/bootstrap shell tests.

Note: the CUJ audit found agentic-m22's close reason didn't record two sub-observations from its own title (the fresh-session /work trigger check, and CUJ-2 hook-block repeat on the consuming repo) -- filed separately (see agentic-49u-era audit follow-ups) rather than reopening this settled epic.

title: agent-console: hardcoded personal service name com.sjaconette.agentprof-pprof
description: agent-console/agent-console.py ~line 3412 hardcodes the personal launchd service name 'com.sjaconette.agentprof-pprof', violating the zero-hardcoded-user rule (~/claude checkout-location fallbacks family, cf agentic-acl). Make it configurable (env/arg) with no personal default. Discovered by the subproject-cluster wave; travels with the agent-console extraction (agentic-2my).

title: decide: extract agentprof + agent-console to sibling repos (consume as binaries)
description: Owner chose PLAN (2026-07-23). Plan written: specs/extract-subprojects/SPEC.md (Status: waiting — execution owner-scheduled: needs new sibling repos + release wiring the toolkit repo cannot create headless). Closing the decision; the spec tracks the execution.

title: consolidate skill trigger-overlap (prose pack caveats, review cluster)
description: Real defect is trigger OVERLAP, not raw count (progressive disclosure makes count cheap). The prose pack (grounding/anti-ai-slop/humanizer/doc-coauthoring/writing-user-docs) is defined largely by mutual do-NOT-use caveats, and 'review this' fires ~9 overlapping verbs across prose-review/critique/code-review/review. Tighten boundaries or collapse (e.g. generate+edit into one prose skill); consolidate review under critique with a target flag. NOT a blanket count cut.

title: remove learn-anything from the shipped toolkit plugin
description: learn-anything is a 367-line personal study tool (Anki/Obsidian) in a repo whose CLAUDE.md declares it IS the agentic-dev toolkit; nothing in the pipeline references it. Relocate to a personal skills dir / drop from the marketplace plugin. (Installed 2026-07-23 with the writing pack; it was the one member that never fit the toolkit's scope.)

title: compress or delete ~750 lines of uncited one-time research docs
description: docs/codebase-context-tree-research, task-tracking-design-research, cross-repo-rollout, research-followups have zero inbound citations from .claude/ or docs/. Their durable lessons already live in the rule/skill they informed. Compress each to a one-line docs/memory.md entry or delete.

title: delete drain_frontier.py (408-line dead scanner of frozen markdown headers)
description: drain_frontier.py scans specs/*/tasks Status headers that AGENTS.md calls 'frozen, no longer scanned'; the live frontier is agentic/frontier.py + bd ready. Delete it or demote to a test fixture against agentic/frontier.py. Confirm no live caller first (shadow.py test references it as the pre-cutover differential — keep that test path working or update it).

title: mechanize completion->archive so specs/ stops accumulating
description: 33 done + 4 obsolete specs sit in live specs/ (every scan walks ~2.6x the live set) because completion never triggers archival. Fix: add a completion->archive step to /drain and /spec-completion-review; tombstone specs/QUEUE.md. Complements agentic-jqq (the one-time manual sweep) with the mechanism that prevents recurrence.

title: split token-discipline.md: 363 always-on lines, no TOC, dead-machinery narration
description: token-discipline.md is 363 lines re-sent every session with no table of contents (violates the repo's own >100-line convention), and multiple always-on paragraphs exist only to narrate deleted swarm/baton/generation machinery. Fix: ~60-line always-on core (tier ladder + delegation defaults) + on-demand reference.md; cut each 'X was deleted so the carve-out no longer applies' clause down to the plain current rule.

title: .claude/runtime.md is a phantom file the tier-routing doctrine depends on
description: token-discipline.md (~line 111) and docs/guides/model-routing.md tell every agent to read .claude/runtime.md for model tier pins, but the file does not exist -> cheap-tier routing dead-ends, agents silently inherit the session model. Fix: create .claude/runtime.md with tier->alias pins OR repoint all refs at runtimes/claude-code.md, and add a doctrine link-check so no clause hinges on a missing path. Cheapest high-leverage fix in the critique.

title: repo holistic critique 2026-07-23: structural findings
description: 6-lens adversarial panel + synthesis. Thesis: real product (idea->critique->build->drain, gate hooks, ctx) buried under a triple-encoded task-state layer (with backwards sync) and a self-narrating doctrine layer. Children are the actionable findings; agentic-uz1 (backwards shadow-sync, P0) and agentic-jqq (spec archival) already track two of them.

title: archive 6 obsolete/done specs, updating their live inbound citations
description:
Cruft audit + citation map (2026-07-23). Archive these obsolete/done specs to specs/archive/, updating inbound citations FIRST (dangling doctrine links are the risk):
- attended-task-human-boundedness (obsolete): refs in specs/agentic-core-redesign/TRIAGE.md, specs/ctx-cujs/DRAIN-BATON.md (both frozen spec history).
- drain-read-once-discipline (obsolete): ref in TRIAGE.md (frozen).
- critique-breakdown-self-chain-gap (obsolete): refs in specs/deterministic-skill-chaining/SPEC.md, TRIAGE.md, docs/external-playbooks.md (LIVE - update this one).
- deterministic-skill-chaining (obsolete): refs in agentic-core-redesign/tasks/02+10, TRIAGE.md, docs/external-playbooks.md (LIVE - update).
- structured-handoff-headers (open, 2/2 done - agentic-7j8): refs in specs/cheap-task-status-checks/SPEC.md, docs/task-tracking-design-research doc, HUMAN.md (LIVE - update). Close/flip its status via bd too.
codequality-agent-console-mutation-coverage already archived (0 refs). Best as a fresh focused session or once agentic-bev's completion->archive automation lands.

title: make ultra the always path for drain; remove lint-ultra-gate; clean repo cruft
description: Owner directive (interactive): drain compiles the ready queue into a Workflow always (no runtime-profile gate, no ultracode opt-in condition); delete evals/lint-ultra-gate.sh and its CLAUDE.md paragraph; de-gate critique/build/idea ultra sections (build keeps a non-workflow verifier path — Workflow nesting is one level only, so a drain-spawned build worker cannot nest a workflow); rewrite the unattended-worker invariant. Plus a repo cruft sweep.

title: decide: .beads/config.yaml commits a machine-local sync.remote (127.0.0.1 proxy)
description: The committed sync.remote pins a container/proxy-local URL unreachable on other machines — the failure docs/memory/agentic-init-remote-override.md already documents. Owner decision: move sync.remote to an uncommitted per-machine layer (env var BD_SYNC_REMOTE or a gitignored config overlay) or accept the per-clone override step. Not changed unilaterally: it is the owner's sync config.

title: docs/memory/root-container-test-failures.md lists the wrong failure set
description: The entry names three chmod-based failures as the known container set, but a fresh root container here hits three PATH-based ones — the memory misleads the exact reader it exists for. Fix: update the entry from the CUJ tester's observed set, keeping the old set as history if still real on macOS.

title: drain cites 'agentic inbox', an unimplemented stub
description: Drain's batch interview names 'agentic inbox / bd list' as the surfacing mechanism; agentic inbox prints 'not implemented'. Fix: drop the stub from drain's text (bd list suffices) or implement inbox; pick when drain's other fixes land.

title: build: first-30-lines window spent on /goal variant, not execution contracts
description: Lines 10-37 cover the optional walk-away mode while the bd claim step, no-runnable-criteria stop rule, and Rigor gate sit past line 50 — the compaction-survival convention inverted. Fix: move the /goal section below step 0 and hoist the execution-critical contracts into the first 30 lines.

title: specs/structured-handoff-headers: stale open, contradicts shipped six-line header
description: Spec still Status: open with both tasks done; text defines a five-line header (no Tracked:) and declares HANDOFF-<topic>.md detection out of scope — both superseded on this branch. Fix: close/archive the spec with a superseded note pointing at handoff/SKILL.md + the hook, and flip its status via shadow-sync.

title: Breakdown-ready: true token: critique/idea promise it, drain never reads it
description: critique:36-40 and idea:202 call the marker 'the token /drain reads to auto-invoke /breakdown', but drain's Auto-breakdown section keys only on 'a critic-READY spec' and never names the token. Fix: pick one contract — drain greps the marker (one line in drain) or critique/idea stop calling it a drain token.

title: bin/check-token-discipline red on committed tree (7 violations)
description: The mechanically-enforced dispatch-authoring check fails on drain/SKILL.md, drain/reference.md, evals/SKILL.md, workflows/deep-research.js while token-discipline.md claims enforcement. Fix: repair each flagged dispatch (tier, budget, loop bound) or amend the checker's expectations where doctrine changed; then wire the check into scripts/check.sh so it can't silently rot again.

title: resume-handoff: 'Tracked: none - bd unavailable' dead-ends when resumer also lacks bd
description: handoff promises resume-time filing, but when the resuming machine also lacks bd the skill skips filing AND consumes the file, losing the parked items' tracker linkage. Fix: when bd is unavailable at resume time, defer consumption (leave the handoff in place, say why) or write the unfiled items into the resumed session's first report as explicit pending-filing lines.

title: workboard: scan_handoffs misses HANDOFF-<topic>.md alternates
description: workboard.py:601-607 patterns tuple matches only literal HANDOFF.md at three depths, so alternate-named handoffs (e.g. the committed .claude/HANDOFF-drain-hub.md) are invisible to the dashboard and dispatch-resume actions, contradicting the widened hook/skill (HANDOFF*.md). Fix: change the patterns to HANDOFF*.md at the same depths + a test_workboard.py case with an alternate-named fixture.

title: breakdown: '- Group:' lines are a dead contract post-cutover
description: breakdown step 5 says 'drain parses group membership from them' and the hand-off claims a Parallel-window:/Group: line opts a queue in, but post-cutover drain/agentic never read Group lines (concurrency = user throughput ask + Touch disjointness) and the grammar spec moved to specs/archive/drain-rolling-window. Fix: rewrite step 5 + hand-off — Touch: header (shadow-synced) is the machine contract, Parallelization section is human rationale; repoint the citation to the archive as history.

title: drain: interrupted-session claims vanish from bd ready (resumability gap)
description: bd ready excludes in_progress, so an issue claimed when a drain session dies never resurfaces; drain also omits /work's .beads/session-claims append/remove, so bd-compliance can't catch the orphan. Fix: drain's claim/close include session-claims bookkeeping, plus a startup sub-step listing in_progress issues with no live session (bd list --status in_progress --json) to unclaim/re-queue before the first ready read. Sandbox-confirmed by the CUJ test.

title: drain: Touch-overlap safety claims a bd feature that does not exist
description: drain SKILL.md asserts bd ready excludes issues whose Touch paths overlap claimed work ('bd computes this') but that logic lives only in agentic/ready.py+frontier.py; a concurrent window off raw 'bd ready --json' can dispatch colliding issues. Fix: route drain's queue-read/claim through 'python3 -m agentic ready --json' and 'python3 -m agentic claim <id>', or delete the claim and require orchestrator-side Touch disjointness checks before concurrent dispatch.

title: adversarial skills/CUJ review 2026-07-23: parent
description: Parent for the confirmed findings of the 79-agent ultracode review (6 lenses + 4 sandboxed CUJ tests, skeptic-verified: 36 confirmed of 69 raw). One-to-three-line doc fixes were applied directly on branch claude/skills-beads-tracking-gaps-ilanej; the issues below carry the behavioral/code fixes. Full findings JSON archived in the session scratchpad.

title: install writing skill pack + example-corpus skills + self-documenting-code rule
description: User request: install uploaded 6-skill writing pack (grounding, anti-ai-slop-writing, humanizer, doc-coauthoring, writing-user-docs, learn-anything); add writing-examples and code-examples corpus skills (capture bad example, approve better version, grow corpus for future agents); add self-documenting-code rule (comments only on public surface area, elsewhere = smell) to quality-discipline.md; incorporate frontier-lab skill-authoring best practices (research in flight). Design at scratchpad/design-writing-code-corpus.md; Fable validation before implementation per user directive.

title: align handoff machinery with beads-native and frontier-lab handoff doctrine
description: User request (widened mid-session): research how Steve Yegge (beads) and frontier labs do handoffs AND how frontier labs verify agent task completion; align this repo's handoff machinery and verification skills/workflow with the findings. Research via /factcheck (5 cited source-cluster workers: beads/Yegge, Anthropic handoffs, OpenAI+Cognition handoffs, Anthropic verification, OpenAI verification). Alignment lands in docs/external-playbooks.md plus skill edits citing it.

title: decide: wire hooks/bd-bootstrap/ensure-bd.sh into .claude/settings.json SessionStart
description: The hook (shipped, tested, opt-in) replaces the bare 'bd prime --hook-json' entry so fresh containers self-hydrate (install bd if missing, bd init+import from committed JSONL, then prime) instead of silently running outside the tracker. Owner declined the direct settings.json edit during the gaps session - needs an explicit yes plus the timeout: 300 on the entry. One-line change described in hooks/bd-bootstrap/README.md.

title: consolidate stray HANDOFF-*.md files into bd-tracked state or delete
description: On the primary machine: .claude/HANDOFF-ctx-review.md and .claude/HANDOFF-task-tracking-specs.md (untracked), .claude/HANDOFF.md.stale-pathspec-commit-hardening (parked stale), plus committed .claude/HANDOFF-drain-hub.md whose thread is DONE. Each needs its pending state filed in bd (per handoff SKILL.md's new Tracked: contract) and the file consumed/deleted. The handoff-resume hook now flags HANDOFF*.md so the committed ones will surface at session start; the untracked ones exist only on the primary machine.

title: md<->bd status vocabulary mismatch: deferred/obsolete have no bd counterpart
description: specs/*/tasks Status headers use deferred (4) and obsolete (26) which shadow-sync has no bd status mapping for; bd shows only open/blocked/closed. Decide the mapping (e.g. deferred->blocked with note, obsolete->closed-wontfix) and encode it in agentic/shadow.py so counts reconcile across the two surfaces.

title: skills/beads tracking gaps: wire bd into pipeline skills, handoff strays, remote bootstrap
description: User-reported: skills and beads not tracking everything; handoffs still ad hoc. This branch (claude/skills-beads-tracking-gaps-ilanej) wires bd into handoff/resume-handoff/breakdown/build/qa-sweep/distill, widens the handoff-resume hook to HANDOFF*.md, registers the missing agentic shadow-sync CLI subcommand, and adds an opt-in bd-bootstrap SessionStart hook for containers without the bd binary.

title: ~/claude's own Stop hook doesn't run scripts/check.sh
description: CLAUDE.md's Commits section (and this repo's own /gate skill, which teaches other repos this exact pattern) documents a two-layer gate: fast pre-commit staged-file checks, plus a Stop hook that runs the full scripts/check.sh before work can be called done. .claude/settings.json's own Stop hooks run plugin-autorefresh and bd-compliance.sh only -- never scripts/check.sh. Found during the 2026-07-23 critical repo audit: the toolkit that teaches verification-first gating to consuming repos doesn't dogfood it on itself. Needs a decision on cost tradeoff first (scripts/check.sh's pytest suite alone runs ~107s), not a blind hook add -- e.g. gate only on files-changed, or accept the latency.

title: workboard: live server hangs on /workboard
description: Diagnosed (cluster wave): NO deadlock/infinite hang exists — _board_lock is never re-entered and every subprocess is timeout-bounded. The '/workboard hang' is unbounded LATENCY: a cold-cache request held _board_lock through a full assemble that stacked a 12s gh re-query (fixed: mh1 negative cache) + a duplicate ~10s claude-agents spawn (fixed: tlj dedup) + git×N. The two dominant stalls are removed; residual cold-cache scan latency would need stale-while-revalidate — deferred, travels with the agent-console extraction (agentic-2my).

title: design: decouple the session-refresh guardrail from agentprof's cost-attribution machinery
description: Investigated 2026-07-23 after a hypothesis that agentprof's p90_ctx was folding subagent tokens into main-session context (RULED OUT: internal/costsummary/costsummary.go structurally excludes agent-stack samples; TestBuildSessionsSectionContextPercentilesMainLoopOnly covers exactly this case). Two separate open questions surfaced during that investigation, maintainer-raised: (1) is cache_read_tokens+input_tokens per model call actually the right proxy for what degrades attention/costs a re-prime, or just a convenient one -- worth scrutiny, not verified deeply; (2) should hooks/session-refresh/refresh-check.sh keep depending on agentprof's --since-windowed cost-summary machinery at all, versus a simpler, more direct read of the CURRENT session's own live context size (context size and token-use-for-cost-attribution are different though related problems -- agentprof should stay the deep tool for the latter; the hook just needs 'a reasonable guardrail' for the former, cheaply and reliably). Candidate outcome: a simpler/more direct context-size source for the hook specifically, decoupled from agentprof's session-summary computation, while agentprof keeps owning richer cost/cache/skill-attribution analysis.
close_reason: done: hooks/session-refresh/refresh-check.sh redesigned to read transcript_path directly off the hook's own stdin payload, no agentprof dependency (commit 2a563440); agentprof retained separately for general cost-attribution digging

title: docs/TASKS.md needs a stale-entry sweep + bd conversion
description: docs/TASKS.md is a dated tech-debt journal (not checkbox-based, unlike other repos' docs/TASKS.md), so it wasn't in scope for task 05's shadow-sync (specs/*/tasks/*.md only) or the cross-repo checkbox conversion. At least 2 entries are now moot post-pivot: 'drain/SKILL.md length rebound: 520 lines' (it's 114 lines after task 09's rewrite) and 'Two independent Touch-disjointness predicate implementations' (both drain_frontier.py and touch_disjoint.py/admission.py, the two sides of that entry, are now deleted). Needs: read every entry, close/remove what the pivot already resolved, file the rest as real bd issues, then decide whether the file itself gets retired in favor of bd or kept as a lighter-weight ideas scratchpad.
close_reason: Swept: removed 5 pivot-mooted entries (2 stale drain-line-count entries superseded by the 114-line rewrite, the touch_disjoint.py duplicate-implementation entry since that file was deleted, the attended-gen-1-baton entry since baton machinery is deleted, and the drain_frontier.py draft/obsolete-status bug since that vocabulary is already accepted in code). Filed the remaining 14 real entries as bd issues (discovered-from:agentic-49u). Retired the file itself: agent-console.py already stopped scanning it ('tracking retired with scan_tasks'), and CLAUDE.md now makes bd the sole tracker -- replaced its content with a short pointer to bd.

title: task 09 gap: list_specs.py and prioritize_scan.py never re-pointed onto bd
description: Task 09's own Goal named these as readers to 're-point onto bd... or retire' alongside drain_frontier.py/status.sh, but its actual implementation only touched status.sh + drain's baton/lease machinery. .claude/skills/list-specs/list_specs.py and .claude/skills/prioritize/prioritize_scan.py still scan markdown Status: headers directly, unaware bd is now the source of truth (shadow-synced from markdown, not the reverse). Either re-point both onto bd (bd list --json) like status.sh was, or retire the /list-specs and /prioritize skills if /work + bd ready supersede them.
close_reason: Retired /list-specs and /prioritize skills (commit 4e5ed866) rather than re-pointing their scanners onto bd. bd list --json / bd ready + /work supersede them per the 2026-07-22 pivot's 'what goes away' table. Deleted both skill dirs, their scanners+tests, the evals/prioritize evalset, and the empty antigravity mirror dirs; swept all live-wiring references (execution-stage enumerations, shared-header docstrings, COVERAGE.md rows, bin/human-followups paid-eval step); bumped plugin.json to 0.13.0. scripts/check.sh green.

title: breakdown/SKILL.md: stale mirror-check + baton-generation-budget references
description: Line 127 tells breakdown to check a mirror between .claude/skills/*/SKILL.md and antigravity/.agents/workflows/*.md — that tree was deleted (core task 10). Lines 205-206 describe tuning /drain's baton self-relaunch generation budget — that machinery was deleted (core task 09). Both are stale post-pivot doc drift outside task 14's Touch (docs/*.md only, not skill files).
close_reason: Fixed: removed the antigravity/codex mirror-collision example and the antigravity Touch-path mirror-check paragraph (both describe deleted mirror trees) from breakdown/SKILL.md; also removed the dead 'Queue tuning' baton/Relaunch-every section.

title: core task 15 cross-repo rollout
description: Propagate the pivot to other repos on this machine (bd/plugin currency audit + HUMAN.md filing). See specs/agentic-core-redesign/tasks/15-cross-repo-rollout.md
close_reason: task 15 verified: all acceptance commands pass (phase-2 workflow sweep)

title: core task 14 doc currency sweep
description: Doc currency sweep — docs/architecture.md, anthropic-playbook.md, external-playbooks.md, decisions/work-tracking.md, memory.md. See specs/agentic-core-redesign/tasks/14-doc-currency-sweep.md
close_reason: task 14 verified: all acceptance commands pass (phase-2 workflow sweep)

title: CUJ-5: run the documented install on a real consuming repo (ynab-mcp-server) — repeat CUJ-1/CUJ-2 there; also observe the fresh-session /work trigger
close_reason: done: cross-repo-beads-adoption workflow performed the live bd install on ynab-mcp-server 2026-07-23 (13 issues created, verified non-lossy, pushed) — satisfies CUJ-5

title: critic F2-F4: preflight wording (agent-count gate), screen-stub file+exit2, plugin.json bump
close_reason: preflight reworded to agent-count gate (skill + script header), screen-stub step documents file input + exit 2, plugin.json 0.9.32->0.10.0

title: dead-code removal across the new artifacts
close_reason: no dead code found — two independent passes (mine + critic) over all new scripts

title: eval scenario evals/work/01-queue-discipline
close_reason: reviewed; scenario passes end-to-end via stub runner (1/1 PASS)

title: onboard extension, gate reference, AGENTS.md naming /work
close_reason: reviewed; duplicate codex block removed from AGENTS.md, /work named as attended default

title: Land ctx-skill-token-doctrine registry slots for ctx-doc-drift-gate + ctx-output-shape-gaps
description:
Three P3 tasks have sat blocked since 2026-07-21: specs/ctx-doc-drift-gate/tasks/02 and /03, and specs/ctx-output-shape-gaps/tasks/03. Each Unblock check greps specs/ctx-skill-token-doctrine/SPEC.md for a registry slot (ctx-doc-drift-gate / ctx-output-shape-gaps) that was never landed there — confirmed absent by direct grep 2026-07-24. Recovered from a stray, never-consumed .claude/HANDOFF-ctx-review.md left by a 2026-07-21 session; everything else in that handoff (6 other ctx specs) has since gone done/obsolete through normal drain/build work, so this registry-landing step is the only genuinely open remnant.

Before landing the registry commit: verify whether the DRAIN-OWNER.md lease machinery these tasks' Unblock notes reference (e.g. specs/ctx-cujs/DRAIN-OWNER.md gen 7) is still meaningful post agentic-core-redesign pivot — CLAUDE.md now states baton/lease/generation/tournament/swarm apparatus was deleted in that cutover, yet 20 DRAIN-OWNER.md files (including ctx-cujs's) still exist on disk. That inconsistency should be resolved (or at minimum noted) before trusting the lease-gated Landing order instructions in specs/ctx-doc-drift-gate/SPEC.md and specs/ctx-output-shape-gaps/SPEC.md.

title: specs/beads-daily-skill: no automated test for claimed-open-issue Stop-hook block
description: Found during the 2026-07-23 CUJ audit: specs/beads-daily-skill's acceptance criterion 'hook test: claimed-open bd issue -> Stop hook blocks' has no automated test. tests/ contains nothing touching session-claims; test_install_gates.sh covers installation only. The block behavior has only ever been exercised as a one-time manual transcript, so a regression here would go undetected. Add a shell test that claims a bd issue, simulates a Stop event, and asserts the hook exits non-zero / blocks.

title: ynab-mcp-server: beads-daily-skill consuming-repo install incomplete
description:
Found during the 2026-07-23 CUJ audit while re-verifying specs/beads-daily-skill's CUJ-5/CUJ-6 evidence against the actual consuming repo (~/ynab-mcp-new, formerly ynab-mcp-server).
- CUJ-5 gap: no Bash(bd *) allowlist anywhere in .claude/settings.json -- the spec itself documents that a missing bd allowlist 'measurably killed tool adoption'.
- CUJ-6 gap: no .beads/issues.jsonl on disk or committed in that repo, so CUJ-6's claim ('any agent that can read a file can read the queue') doesn't hold there, and the documented install step 3 ('commit the export') was never followed.
Fix in the ynab-mcp-server/ynab-mcp-new repo itself, not here.

title: ctx-cujs/beads-daily-skill: evidence and doc staleness sweep
description:
Found during the 2026-07-23 CUJ/objectives audit, four related staleness items:
1. docs/guides/ctx-cujs.md:149-160's 'anti-gaming, evidence-backed' Gap table is stale in 5 of 8 rows (show, the absence-boundary output at cmd/no_match.rs, refs --zone, the survey scout recipe, and the file-scoped selector all shipped but are still listed 'specced, not shipped').
2. specs/beads-daily-skill/evidence/cuj-5-second-repo.md says 'Status: MANUAL-PENDING' while bd issue agentic-m22 (the task that ran this CUJ) was closed done on 2026-07-23 -- the evidence file was never updated to match.
3. agentic-m22's own close reason didn't record two sub-observations named in its own title: the fresh-session /work trigger check, and the CUJ-2 hook-block repeat on the consuming repo.
4. specs/ctx-cujs/SPEC.md R3's acceptance criterion greps for 'the antigravity mirror', which is unsatisfiable as written now that the mirror trees are deleted (2026-07-22 pivot) -- the spec was never amended after that pivot.

title: agent-console: test gaps beyond the mutation-endpoints spec
description: parse_repos is only ever mocked, never tested directly; apply_priority's insert-after-H1 branch is untested.

title: agent-console: doc drift (docstring, README cache TTL, env naming)
description: Docstring still says 'Run: skills-dashboard.py' (agent-console.py:14); README claims board cache '~20s' vs BOARD_TTL = 45 (:310); env naming split AGENT_CONSOLE_PORT (install.sh) vs runtime SKILLS_DASHBOARD_PORT. Align all three.

title: agent-console: set_priority invalidates the wrong cache
description: agent-console.py:2879 zeroes _plugins_cache['ts'] (self-described no-op) forcing a needless plugin-list respawn instead of invalidating the board cache it actually affects.

title: agent-console: gh_visibility has no negative caching
description: The cache timestamp advances only on success, so a present-but-failing gh re-runs a 12s-timeout 'gh repo list' on every post-TTL rebuild -- contradicts CLAUDE.md's 'off the hot path'. Cache failures with a shorter TTL.

title: agent-console: duplicate 'claude agents --json' spawn per board rebuild
description: workboard.assemble() fetches it (.claude/skills/workboard/workboard.py:687) and agents_view() fetches it again (agent-console/agent-console.py:541) -- two subprocess spawns, each with an 8s timeout, on every rebuild. Share one fetch per rebuild cycle. (from archived-repo review; the GitHub sticklane/agent-console repo is an archived stale snapshot -- all fixes land in ~/claude)

title: implementation-worker.md has no self-contained output budget
description: scout (<=300 words), verifier ('under a page'), and critic all bound their output per the conventions; implementation-worker defers entirely to the dispatch prompt. Add a fallback cap or document the dispatch-prompt-owns-it decision in the agent file.

title: ~/claude checkout-location fallbacks in shipping code
description: workboard.py:1248 falls back to Path.home()/'claude/agentprof/agentprof' and bin/sync-workflows:18 defaults SRC to $HOME/claude/.claude/workflows -- both env-overridable, but a plugin installed elsewhere gets a dead fallback. Derive from the script's own location where possible.

title: distill's model: opus frontmatter vs the no-bare-model convention
description: CLAUDE.md says skills use tier language, 'never a bare model name', but distill/SKILL.md:4 still pins model: opus in frontmatter. Decide: exempt frontmatter model: pins explicitly in the convention (agents already pin aliases by design, e.g. verifier.md's model: sonnet) or route distill through tier language.

title: Normalize Next stage: closers on build and fleet skills
description: build/SKILL.md has no Next stage: line at all despite producing artifacts (evals/SKILL.md:56 shows the execution-stage form), and fleet/SKILL.md:51 uses the wrong label ('Next pipeline step:'). drain's own closer is already fixed. Either add conforming closers to the remaining two or write the execution-stage carve-out into CLAUDE.md's convention.

title: agentprof: collapse cmd_* flag/positional boilerplate
description: The FlagSet-setup -> parsePositionals -> 'expected exactly one ... file' head block is near-identical across cmd_gcp.go:14-26, cmd_vertex.go:13-24, cmd_otel.go:31-41, and the -o flag string is repeated verbatim in five files; run_adapter.go already collapsed the shared tail — add the companion head helper.

title: agent-console: collapse duplicated row/section rendering
description: The line/trunc/meta row skeleton is hand-built at agent-console.py:1933, :1984, :1992, :2000, and the three detail-page renderers (render_repo_page:1193, render_session_page:1380, render_spec_page:1442) each rebuild the same parts=[] + section-div scaffold. A row(...) and section(title, html) helper pair removes the copies.

title: agent-console: name the magic numbers
description: Scattered literals — panel top-N [:5] (agent-console.py:1930), prompt truncation [:90] (:2000), tail_events defaults n=50, window=65_536 (:1287), 4s git timeout (:364) — should be named constants (env-configurable only where there's a real reason, matching the existing stop-grace model).

title: agent-console: log the silent broad excepts
description: _running_pid_for (agent-console.py:567) and _claude_run_bg (:2334) catch Exception and drop it with no logging, unlike the two commented request-boundary catches — a lookup or repo-scan failure vanishes. Add a debug log line.

title: agent-console: make the pprof URL configurable
description: The sibling agentprof UI is hardcoded as http://127.0.0.1:8901 at agent-console.py:1793 and :2138 while the console's own PORT/HOST are env-configurable; tests/test_profile_links.py:70 pins the literal. Introduce an AGENTPROF_URL-style env default and update the test.

title: workboard: --abandon for stale toolkit specs
description: The abandon-marker mechanism only covers Antigravity conversations; stale specs/<slug> dirs still require manual Status: deferred edits or deletion. Consider a symmetric one-command defer.

title: agentprof: surface pricing-table staleness
description: The rate table's only freshness marker is a comment (internal/pricing/table.go, 'fetched 2026-07-02'), and the table already knowingly diverges (Sonnet's introductory $2/$10 through 2026-08-31 'is not modeled') with nothing flagging it at runtime. Add a fetched-date constant surfaced in summary output plus a documented refresh procedure; pricing_test.go only re-does the arithmetic, so it can never catch drift from real prices.

title: workboard: surface per-session token/cost
description: Anthropic's multi-agent research guidance (docs/agent-dashboards.md) says cost belongs on any agent-management surface — token usage explains ~80% of performance variance. Session records may carry usage data; if so, add a cost column to the sessions tables and a total tile.

title: drain/SKILL.md's 'Launch authorization' section may contradict CLAUDE.md's 'contract blocks are gone' claim
description: CLAUDE.md's authoring conventions say core task 11 retired the per-skill launch-authorization contract and 'the contract blocks are gone from the SKILL.md files.' But task 09 (which ran after 11, rewriting drain/SKILL.md 496->114 lines) left/reintroduced a paragraph headed '**Launch authorization (execution stage).**' in drain/SKILL.md (lines ~15-19). It's a short citation to .claude/rules/untrusted-data.md, not a restated full contract, so it may be fine as a brief pointer -- but it's worth a maintainer read to decide: trim to a bare citation with no heading, or is CLAUDE.md's 'gone from the SKILL.md files' claim itself now inaccurate and needs softening.
close_reason: Resolved: trimmed drain/SKILL.md's 'Launch authorization' block from a headed mini-contract to a one-sentence citation of untrusted-data.md, so CLAUDE.md's 'contract blocks are gone from the SKILL.md files' claim now holds.

title: workboard/SKILL.md: stale 'batons' reference (line 58)
description: References drain batons surfacing in workboard's needs-attention inbox — that machinery was deleted (core task 09). Stale post-pivot doc drift outside task 14's Touch (docs/*.md only, not skill files).
close_reason: Fixed: workboard/SKILL.md's needs-attention-inbox exception now names the parked-HANDOFF.md case instead of the deleted drain-batons mechanism.

title: install-gates: HOOK_TEMPLATES relies on word-splitting
description: The 'for h in $HOOK_TEMPLATES' loops (bin/install-gates:586, :617) word-split a fixed literal — safe today, the one shellcheck (SC2086) flag in bin/; a bash array is future-proof.
